perturbation.original_text: 3. Recipients shall use Confidential Information solely for the purpose (the "Permitted Purpose") specified at the end of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. Recipients shall use Confidential Information primarily for the purpose (the "Permitted Purpose") specified at the end of this Agreement, but may also use it for related internal business analysis.
perturbation.explanation: The change from 'solely' to 'primarily' and the addition of 'but may also use it for related internal business analysis' broadens the scope of permitted use. This is a contradiction with the definition of the Permitted Purpose at the end of the agreement and changes the intent of the contract. Now the recipient could do more than evaluate a joint venture, which could violate section 8.
perturbation.contradicted_text: A. The "Permitted Purpose" with respect to Confidential Information disclosed to the Company shall be, to evaluate whether or not to enter into an agreement with Ben & Jerry's relating to a joint venture, other cooperative venture or the like between Ben & Jerry's and the Company.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated the Confidential Information should be used 'solely' for the Permitted Purpose, which is defined as evaluating a joint venture with Ben & Jerry's. The changed text broadens the use of Confidential Information to 'primarily' for the Permitted Purpose and adds 'related internal business analysis'. This directly contradicts the definition of the Permitted Purpose in section A, creating an in-text contradiction because now the recipient could do more than evaluate a joint venture.

perturbation.original_text: 6. Recipient agrees not to reproduce or copy by any means Confidential Information, except as reasonably required to accomplish such Recipient's Permitted Purpose. Upon written demand by Discloser at any time, Recipient shall return promptly to Discloser or destroy, at Recipient's option, all tangible materials that disclose or embody Confidential Information except that Recipient's counsel may, upon written notice to Disclosure's counsel, retain a single copy of Confidential Information in its records solely for use in dispute resolution or as otherwise required by law. Recipient shall not remove any proprietary rights legend from, and shall, upon Discloser's reasonable written request, add an appropriate proprietary legend to, materials disclosing or embodying Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. Recipient agrees not to reproduce or copy by any means Confidential Information, except as required. Upon written demand by Discloser only within the first year of this agreement, Recipient shall return or destroy tangible materials that disclose Confidential Information as requested. Recipient's counsel can retain copies indefinitely. Recipient will make a reasonable effort not to remove any proprietary rights legend from materials disclosing or embodying Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: Multiple changes are introduced here: 'except as reasonably required to accomplish such Recipient's Permitted Purpose' to 'except as required' broadens the exception. 'at any time' to 'only within the first year of this agreement' limits the timeframe. Replacing the obligation to return promptly with only 'return' removes the time bound, and 'can retain copies indefinitely' contradicts 'solely for use in dispute resolution or as otherwise required by law' and changing 'shall not remove any proprietary rights legend' to 'Recipient will make a reasonable effort not to remove any proprietary rights legend' reduces the obligation. Specifically, section 9 which concerns the timeframe and modifications to the contract is being contradicted by this change, since it has a conflicting time frame.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9. The rights and obligations herein shall bind the parties and their successors and assigns. This Agreement expresses the entire agreement and understanding of the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes al prior oral or written agreements, commitments and understandings pertaining to the subject matter hereof. Any modifications of or changes in this Agreement shall be in writing and signed by both parties. This Agreement shall remain in full force and effect for two years. Any causes of action accrued on or before the expiration of such period shall survive the expiration of the applicable statute of limitations.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in section 6 introduces contradictions with section 9. Section 6 limits the timeframe for the Discloser to demand the return of confidential information to within the first year, whereas section 9 states the agreement remains in effect for two years. This creates a contradiction regarding the timeframe obligations. Additionally, the original text dictates any modifications must be in writing and signed by both parties as stated in Section 9, whereas the changes made in Section 6 are unilaterally altered and therefore contradicts the previous written agreement detailed in section 9, making it a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 7. In the event that Recipient is ordered to disclose Discloser's Confidential Information pursuant to a judicial or governmental request, requirement or order, Recipient shall promptly notify Discloser and take reasonable steps at Discloser's expense to assist Discloser in contesting such request, requirement or order or otherwise protecting Discloser's rights.
perturbation.changed_text: 7. In the event that Recipient is ordered to disclose Discloser's Confidential Information pursuant to a judicial or governmental request, requirement or order, Recipient shall notify Discloser if deemed necessary by legal counsel. Actions taken to contest such requests are solely at the Recipient's discretion and expense.
perturbation.explanation: The change from 'shall promptly notify' to 'shall notify Discloser if deemed necessary by legal counsel' gives the legal counsel the power to decide, reducing the requirement of notification and shifting the cost of contesting any request to the recipient and giving full discretions. This contradicts the provision in Section 2 of a public release or disclosure of any <PAGE> 2 contemplated transaction shall be made except by a mutually agreed disclosure except that each party may make such disclosure if advised by its outside securities counsel in writing that such disclosure is required.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Except as expressly permitted herein, Recipient shall not disclose Confidential Information and shall prevent the disclosure of such information by Recipient or its affiliates and their respective employees, directors, counsel, advisors, agents and independent contractors (all of which collectively "Representatives") by using the same degree of care to keep confidential the Confidential Information as it uses to keep confidential its own confidential information. In addition, except as permitted herein, Recipient shall not disclose the fact that the parties are exchanging Confidential Information and having discussions. In connection therewith, it is agreed that no public release or disclosure of any <PAGE> 2 contemplated transaction shall be made except by a mutually agreed disclosure except that each party may make such disclosure if advised by its outside securities counsel in writing that such disclosure is required; PROVIDED, HOWEVER, that in such event such party will notify the other party that it intends, as a preliminary matter, to take such action and the outside securities counsel of such party shall first discuss the mater with the outside securities counsel of the other party before any definitive decision is made on the disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates prompt notification to the Discloser in case of a judicial order to disclose confidential information. The changed text modifies this to require notification only if deemed necessary by legal counsel, effectively reducing the Discloser's control over the situation. Furthermore, the original text states that contesting such requests is at the Discloser's expense, while the changed text shifts this responsibility and discretion entirely to the Recipient. This contradicts Section 2, which requires mutual agreement for any public release or disclosure, with a specific process involving outside securities counsel discussion. The changed text in Section 7 allows the Recipient's legal counsel to unilaterally decide on disclosure necessity, bypassing the mutual agreement and counsel discussion required in Section 2, creating a strong inconsistency and contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 9. The rights and obligations herein shall bind the parties and their successors and assigns. This Agreement expresses the entire agreement and understanding of the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes al prior oral or written agreements, commitments and understandings pertaining to the subject matter hereof. Any modifications of or changes in this Agreement shall be in writing and signed by both parties. This Agreement shall remain in full force and effect for two years. Any causes of action accrued on or before the expiration of such period shall survive the expiration of the applicable statute of limitations.
perturbation.changed_text: 9. The rights and obligations herein shall bind the parties and their successors and assigns, except for obligations related to confidentiality, which do not bind successors. This Agreement supersedes all prior agreements, unless expressly referenced herein. Any modifications can be made verbally and confirmed via email. This Agreement shall remain in effect indefinitely, unless terminated with 30 days' notice. Any causes of action will not survive the termination of the agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Several modifications are introduced here: 'except for obligations related to confidentiality, which do not bind successors' contradicts the initial binding statement. 'unless expressly referenced herein' creates ambiguity about what agreements are superseded. 'Any modifications can be made verbally and confirmed via email' contradicts 'shall be in writing and signed by both parties'. 'This Agreement shall remain in effect indefinitely, unless terminated with 30 days' notice' contradicts the two year expiration. 'Any causes of action will not survive the termination of the agreement' contradicts 'Any causes of action accrued on or before the expiration of such period shall survive the expiration of the applicable statute of limitations'. Section 10 concerning governing law is directly contradicted because the contract doesn't have an expiration time, which could create a problem with the laws of the State of New York
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10. This Agreement and any controversies, disputes or claims arising out of or relating to the subject matter of this Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of New York and the parties agree to submit any and all such controversies, disputes, or claims to the exclusive jurisdiction of the federal courts located in New York, New York.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in section 9 creates multiple contradictions with the contradicted text in section 10. Specifically, the original text implies a two-year contract term, whereas the changed text states an indefinite term unless terminated with 30 days' notice. This directly contradicts section 10, which refers to the laws of New York governing disputes 'arising out of or relating to the subject matter of this Agreement.' Because the new version allows indefinite term, the governing law, tied to the agreement's expiration, now has no end, creating a contradiction. The original text also states that any changes to the agreement must be in writing, where the changed text says the modifications can be made verbally, creating more in-text contradiction.
